### Evidence 4099: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/06_POD Design/107_Jobs.extraction.md::E17
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 06_POD Design / 107_Jobs.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Let's just replicate the same with Kubernetes.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4100: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/06_POD Design/107_Jobs.extraction.md::E18
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 06_POD Design / 107_Jobs.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: We will create a pod definition file to perform the same operation.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4101: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/06_POD Design/107_Jobs.extraction.md::E19
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 06_POD Design / 107_Jobs.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: When the pod is created, it runs a container, performs the computation task and exits, and the pod goes into a completed state.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4102: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/06_POD Design/107_Jobs.extraction.md::E20
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 06_POD Design / 107_Jobs.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: But it then recreates the container in an attempt to leave it running.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4103: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/06_POD Design/107_Jobs.extraction.md::E21
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 06_POD Design / 107_Jobs.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Again, the container performs the required computation task and exits.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4104: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/06_POD Design/107_Jobs.extraction.md::E22
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 06_POD Design / 107_Jobs.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And Kubernetes continues to bring it up again.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4105: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/06_POD Design/107_Jobs.extraction.md::E23
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 06_POD Design / 107_Jobs.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And this continues to happen until a threshold is reached.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4106: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/06_POD Design/107_Jobs.extraction.md::E24
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 06_POD Design / 107_Jobs.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So why does that happen?
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4107: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/06_POD Design/107_Jobs.extraction.md::E25
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 06_POD Design / 107_Jobs.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Kubernetes wants your applications to live forever.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4108: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/06_POD Design/107_Jobs.extraction.md::E26
- Type: Operational Insight
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 06_POD Design / 107_Jobs.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: The default behavior of pods is to attempt to restart the container in an effort to keep it running.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4109: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/06_POD Design/107_Jobs.extraction.md::E27
- Type: Operational Insight
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 06_POD Design / 107_Jobs.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: This behavior is defined by the property restart policy set on the pod, which is by default set to always.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Platform Engineering

### Evidence 4110: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/06_POD Design/107_Jobs.extraction.md::E28
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 06_POD Design / 107_Jobs.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And that is why the pod always recreates the container when it exits.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4111: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/06_POD Design/107_Jobs.extraction.md::E29
- Type: Troubleshooting
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 06_POD Design / 107_Jobs.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: You can override this behavior by setting this property to never or on failure.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Troubleshooting

### Evidence 4112: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/06_POD Design/107_Jobs.extraction.md::E30
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 06_POD Design / 107_Jobs.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: That way Kubernetes does not restart the container once the job is finished.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4113: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/06_POD Design/107_Jobs.extraction.md::E31
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 06_POD Design / 107_Jobs.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Now, that works just fine.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4114: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/06_POD Design/107_Jobs.extraction.md::E32
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 06_POD Design / 107_Jobs.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: We have new use cases for batch processing.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4115: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/06_POD Design/107_Jobs.extraction.md::E33
- Type: Exam Tip
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 06_POD Design / 107_Jobs.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: We have large datasets that require multiple pods to process the data in parallel.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4116: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/06_POD Design/107_Jobs.extraction.md::E34
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 06_POD Design / 107_Jobs.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: We wanna make sure that all pods perform the task assigned to them successfully and then exit.

### Evidence 4117: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/06_POD Design/107_Jobs.extraction.md::E35
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 06_POD Design / 107_Jobs.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So we need a manager that can create as many pods as we want to get a work done and ensure that work gets done successfully.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4118: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/06_POD Design/107_Jobs.extraction.md::E36
- Type: Exam Tip
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 06_POD Design / 107_Jobs.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: But we have learned about replica sets that help us create multiple pods.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4119: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/06_POD Design/107_Jobs.extraction.md::E37
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 06_POD Design / 107_Jobs.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: While a replica set is used to make sure a specified number of pods are running at all times, a job is used to run a set of pods to perform a given task to completion.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4448: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/08_State Persistence/134_Why Stateful Sets_.extraction.md::E37
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 08_State Persistence / 134_Why Stateful Sets_.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Now, in the Kubernetes world, we have learned so far that such kind of a deployment in each of these instances, including the master and slaves, are a POD, part of a deployment.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 4449: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/08_State Persistence/134_Why Stateful Sets_.extraction.md::E43
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 08_State Persistence / 134_Why Stateful Sets_.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: With deployments, all PODs, part of the deployment come up at the same time.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 4450: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/08_State Persistence/134_Why Stateful Sets_.extraction.md::E45
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 08_State Persistence / 134_Why Stateful Sets_.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: In the following steps while cloning data from master to slave one or slave one to slave two or while enabling continuous replication on the slaves in steps two, three, five, six, and seven, we must be able to differentiate the master and slave PODs from each other.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4451: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/08_State Persistence/134_Why Stateful Sets_.extraction.md::E46
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 08_State Persistence / 134_Why Stateful Sets_.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So we want to designate one of these PODs as master and others as slave.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4452: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/08_State Persistence/134_Why Stateful Sets_.extraction.md::E48
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 08_State Persistence / 134_Why Stateful Sets_.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: We cannot rely on the IP address as we know by now that IP addresses of PODs are dynamically assigned and they may change when the POD gets recreated.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4453: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/08_State Persistence/134_Why Stateful Sets_.extraction.md::E50
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 08_State Persistence / 134_Why Stateful Sets_.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: As we have seen while working with deployments, the PODs come up with random names, so that won't help us here.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 4454: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/08_State Persistence/134_Why Stateful Sets_.extraction.md::E51
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 08_State Persistence / 134_Why Stateful Sets_.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Even if we decide to designate one of these PODs as the master and use its name to configure the slaves, if that POD crashes and the deployment creates a new POD in its place, it's going to come up with a totally new POD name.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 4455: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/08_State Persistence/134_Why Stateful Sets_.extraction.md::E55
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 08_State Persistence / 134_Why Stateful Sets_.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Stateful sets are similar to deployment sets as in they create PODs based on a template.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 4456: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/08_State Persistence/134_Why Stateful Sets_.extraction.md::E58
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 08_State Persistence / 134_Why Stateful Sets_.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: With stateful sets, PODs are created in a sequential order.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4457: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/08_State Persistence/134_Why Stateful Sets_.extraction.md::E59
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 08_State Persistence / 134_Why Stateful Sets_.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: After the first POD is deployed, it must be in a running and ready state before the next POD is deployed.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4458: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/08_State Persistence/134_Why Stateful Sets_.extraction.md::E62
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 08_State Persistence / 134_Why Stateful Sets_.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Stateful sets assign a unique original index to each POD, a number starting from zero for the first POD and increments by one.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4459: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/08_State Persistence/134_Why Stateful Sets_.extraction.md::E63
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 08_State Persistence / 134_Why Stateful Sets_.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Each POD gets a unique name derived from this index combined with the stateful set name.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4460: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/08_State Persistence/134_Why Stateful Sets_.extraction.md::E64
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 08_State Persistence / 134_Why Stateful Sets_.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So the first POD gets MySQL dash zero, the second gets MySQL dash one, the third gets MySQL dash two, et cetera.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4461: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/08_State Persistence/134_Why Stateful Sets_.extraction.md::E67
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 08_State Persistence / 134_Why Stateful Sets_.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: You can be sure that the first POD in any stateful set is always going to be the name of the stateful set followed by dash zero.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4462: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/08_State Persistence/134_Why Stateful Sets_.extraction.md::E69
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 08_State Persistence / 134_Why Stateful Sets_.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: The POD MySQL dash two knows that it has to perform an initial clone of data from the POD MySQL dash one.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4463: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/08_State Persistence/134_Why Stateful Sets_.extraction.md::E70
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 08_State Persistence / 134_Why Stateful Sets_.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Say if you were to scale up the deployment by deploying another POD, MySQL dash three, for instance, then it would know that it can perform a clone from MySQL dash two.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 4464: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/08_State Persistence/134_Why Stateful Sets_.extraction.md::E72
- Type: Troubleshooting
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 08_State Persistence / 134_Why Stateful Sets_.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Even if the master fails and the POD is recreated, it would still come up with the same name.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Troubleshooting

### Evidence 4465: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/08_State Persistence/134_Why Stateful Sets_.extraction.md::E73
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 08_State Persistence / 134_Why Stateful Sets_.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Stateful sets maintain a sticky identity for each of their PODs, and these help with the remaining steps.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

- Source: learn-kubernetes / 03_Kubernetes Concepts / 014_Pods.txt

- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 03_Kubernetes Concepts / 014_Pods.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: What if the user base further increases and your current node has no sufficient capacity?

### Evidence 4729: learn-kubernetes/03_Kubernetes Concepts/014_Pods.extraction.md::E21
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 03_Kubernetes Concepts / 014_Pods.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Well, then you can always deploy additional pods on a new node in the cluster.

### Evidence 4730: learn-kubernetes/03_Kubernetes Concepts/014_Pods.extraction.md::E22
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 03_Kubernetes Concepts / 014_Pods.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: You will have a new node added to the cluster to expand the cluster's physical capacity.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Architecture

### Evidence 4731: learn-kubernetes/03_Kubernetes Concepts/014_Pods.extraction.md::E23
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 03_Kubernetes Concepts / 014_Pods.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So what I'm trying to illustrate in this slide is that pods usually have a 1 to 1 relationship, with containers running your application.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Platform Engineering

### Evidence 4732: learn-kubernetes/03_Kubernetes Concepts/014_Pods.extraction.md::E24
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 03_Kubernetes Concepts / 014_Pods.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: To scale up, you create new pods, and to scale down you delete existing pods.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4733: learn-kubernetes/03_Kubernetes Concepts/014_Pods.extraction.md::E25
- Type: Warning/Pitfall
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 03_Kubernetes Concepts / 014_Pods.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: You do not add additional containers to an existing pod to scale your application.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4734: learn-kubernetes/03_Kubernetes Concepts/014_Pods.extraction.md::E26
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 03_Kubernetes Concepts / 014_Pods.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Also, if you're wondering how we implement all of this and how we achieve load balancing between containers, etc., we will get into all of that in a later lecture.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4735: learn-kubernetes/03_Kubernetes Concepts/014_Pods.extraction.md::E27
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 03_Kubernetes Concepts / 014_Pods.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: For now, we are only trying to understand the basic concepts.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 03_Kubernetes Concepts / 014_Pods.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: We just said that pods usually have a 1 to 1 relationship with the containers.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4737: learn-kubernetes/03_Kubernetes Concepts/014_Pods.extraction.md::E29
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 03_Kubernetes Concepts / 014_Pods.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: But are we restricted to having a single container in a single pod?
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4738: learn-kubernetes/03_Kubernetes Concepts/014_Pods.extraction.md::E30
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 03_Kubernetes Concepts / 014_Pods.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: No.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4739: learn-kubernetes/03_Kubernetes Concepts/014_Pods.extraction.md::E31
- Type: Exam Tip
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 03_Kubernetes Concepts / 014_Pods.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: A single pod can have multiple containers, except for the fact that they're usually not multiple containers of the same kind.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4740: learn-kubernetes/03_Kubernetes Concepts/014_Pods.extraction.md::E32
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 03_Kubernetes Concepts / 014_Pods.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: As we discussed in the previous slide, if our intention was to scale our application, then we would need to create additional pods.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Platform Engineering

### Evidence 4741: learn-kubernetes/03_Kubernetes Concepts/014_Pods.extraction.md::E33
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 03_Kubernetes Concepts / 014_Pods.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: But sometimes you might have a scenario where you have a helper container that might be doing some kind of supporting task for our web application, such as processing a user entered data, processing a file uploaded by the user, etc. and you want these helper containers to live alongside your application container.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4742: learn-kubernetes/03_Kubernetes Concepts/014_Pods.extraction.md::E34
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 03_Kubernetes Concepts / 014_Pods.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: In that case, you can have both of these containers part of the same pod, so that when a new application container is created.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4743: learn-kubernetes/03_Kubernetes Concepts/014_Pods.extraction.md::E35
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 03_Kubernetes Concepts / 014_Pods.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: The helper is also created and when it dies, the helper also dies.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods

### Evidence 4744: learn-kubernetes/03_Kubernetes Concepts/014_Pods.extraction.md::E36
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 03_Kubernetes Concepts / 014_Pods.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Since they are part of the same pod, the two containers can also communicate with each other directly by referring to each other as local host, since they share the same network space.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Networking

### Evidence 4745: learn-kubernetes/03_Kubernetes Concepts/014_Pods.extraction.md::E37
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 03_Kubernetes Concepts / 014_Pods.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Plus, they can easily share the same storage space as well.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Storage

- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 020_Pods with YAML.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript

### Evidence 4856: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/020_Pods with YAML.extraction.md::E22

- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 020_Pods with YAML.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And so names and labels are children of metadata.

### Evidence 4857: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/020_Pods with YAML.extraction.md::E23
- Type: Best Practice
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 020_Pods with YAML.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: The number of spaces before the two properties name and labels doesn't matter, but they should be the same as they are siblings.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Configuration

### Evidence 4858: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/020_Pods with YAML.extraction.md::E24
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 020_Pods with YAML.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: In this case, labels has more spaces on the left than name, and so it is now a child of the name property instead of a sibling, which is incorrect.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Configuration

### Evidence 4859: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/020_Pods with YAML.extraction.md::E25
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 020_Pods with YAML.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Also, the two properties must have more spaces than its parent, which is metadata, so that it's intended to the right a little bit.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Configuration

### Evidence 4860: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/020_Pods with YAML.extraction.md::E26
- Type: Concept

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: In this case, all three of them have the same number of spaces before them, and so they are all siblings, which is not correct under metadata.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Configuration

### Evidence 4861: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/020_Pods with YAML.extraction.md::E27

- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Configuration

### Evidence 4862: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/020_Pods with YAML.extraction.md::E28

### Evidence 5404: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/026_Replication Controllers and ReplicaSets.extraction.md::E85

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: The selector is not a required field in case of a replication controller, but it is still available when you skip it, as we did in the previous slide, it assumes it to be the same as the labels provided in the Pod definition file.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Scheduling, Platform Engineering

- Content: In case of replica set, a user input is required for this property, and it has to be written in the form of match labels as shown here.

### Evidence 5406: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/026_Replication Controllers and ReplicaSets.extraction.md::E87

### Evidence 5443: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/026_Replication Controllers and ReplicaSets.extraction.md::E124
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 026_Replication Controllers and ReplicaSets.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: The first is to update the number of replicas in the definition file to six, then run the kubectl apply command to specify the same file using the f parameter, and that will update the replica set to have six replicas.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Configuration

### Evidence 5444: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/026_Replication Controllers and ReplicaSets.extraction.md::E125

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: The second way to do it is to run the kube control scale command.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Configuration

### Evidence 5445: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/026_Replication Controllers and ReplicaSets.extraction.md::E126

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Use the replicas parameter to provide the new number of replicas, and specify the same file as input.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 5446: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/026_Replication Controllers and ReplicaSets.extraction.md::E127

- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 5447: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/026_Replication Controllers and ReplicaSets.extraction.md::E128

- Content: However, remember that using the file name as input will not result in the number of replicas being updated automatically in the file.

- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Configuration

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Say, for example, you have a web server that needs to be deployed in a production environment.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Configuration, CKA-CKAD

### Evidence 5810: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/030_Deployments.extraction.md::E5
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 030_Deployments.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: You need not one, but many such instances of the web server running for obvious reasons.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 5811: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/030_Deployments.extraction.md::E6
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 030_Deployments.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Secondly, whenever newer versions of application builds become available on the Docker registry, you would like to upgrade your Docker instances seamlessly.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 5812: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/030_Deployments.extraction.md::E7
- Type: Warning/Pitfall
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 030_Deployments.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: However, when you upgrade your instances, you do not want to upgrade all of them at once, as we just did.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 5813: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/030_Deployments.extraction.md::E8
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 030_Deployments.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: This may impact users accessing our applications, so you might want to upgrade them one after the other.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 5814: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/030_Deployments.extraction.md::E9
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 030_Deployments.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And that kind of upgrade is known as rolling updates.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 5815: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/030_Deployments.extraction.md::E10
- Type: Troubleshooting
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 030_Deployments.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Suppose one of the upgrades you performed resulted in an unexpected error, and you're asked to undo the recent change You would like to be able to roll back the changes that were recently carried out.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Troubleshooting

### Evidence 5816: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/030_Deployments.extraction.md::E11
- Type: Warning/Pitfall
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 030_Deployments.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Finally, say, for example, you would like to make multiple changes to your environment, such as upgrading the underlying web server versions, as well as scaling your environment and also modifying the resource allocations, etc. you do not want to apply each change immediately after the command is run.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Configuration, CKA-CKAD

### Evidence 5817: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/030_Deployments.extraction.md::E12

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Instead, you would like to apply a path to your environment, make the changes and then resume so that all the changes are rolled out together.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Configuration

### Evidence 6001: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E1
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: In this lecture we will talk about updates and rollbacks in a deployment.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 6002: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E2
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Before we look at how we upgrade our application, let's try to understand rollouts and versioning in a deployment.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 6003: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E3
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: When you first create a deployment it triggers a rollout.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 6004: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E4
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: A new rollout creates a new replica set, which is recorded as a new deployment revision in the future when the application is upgraded, meaning when the container version is updated to a new one, a new rollout is triggered and a new deployment revision is created, named revision two.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 6005: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E5
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: This helps us keep track of the changes made to our deployment, and enables us to roll back to a previous version of deployment if necessary.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 6006: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E6
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: You can see the status of your rollout by running the command kube control rollout status, followed by the name of the deployment.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Configuration

### Evidence 6007: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E7
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: To see the revisions and history of rollout, run the Kube control rollout history command followed by the deployment name, and this will show you the revisions and history of our deployment.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Configuration

### Evidence 6008: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E8
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: There are two types of deployment strategies.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 6009: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E9
- Type: Exam Tip
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Say for example you have five replicas of your web application instance deployed.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, CKA-CKAD

### Evidence 6010: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E10
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: One way to upgrade these to a newer version is to destroy all of these, and then create newer versions of application instances.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 6011: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E11
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Meaning first destroy the five running instances and then deploy five new instances of the new application version.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 6012: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E12
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: The problem with this, as you can imagine, is that during the period after the older versions are down and before any newer version is up, the application is down and inaccessible to users.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 6013: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E13
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: This strategy is known as the recreate strategy, and thankfully this is not the default deployment strategy.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 6014: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E14
- Type: Warning/Pitfall
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: The second strategy is where we do not destroy all of them at once.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 6015: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E15
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Instead, we take down the older version and bring up a newer version one by one.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 6016: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E16
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: This way the application never goes down and the upgrade is seamless.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 6017: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E17
- Type: Warning/Pitfall
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Remember, if you do not specify a strategy while creating the deployment, it will assume it to be rolling.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 6018: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E18
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Update.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 6019: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E19
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: In other words, rolling update is the default deployment strategy.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 6020: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E20
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So we talked about upgrades.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 6021: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E21
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: How exactly do you update your deployment?
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 6022: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E22
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: When I say update it could be different things, such as updating your application version by updating the version of Docker containers used, updating their labels, or updating the number of replicas, etc. since we already have a deployment definition file, it is easy for us to modify this file once we make the necessary changes.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 6023: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E23
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: We run the kube control apply command to apply the changes.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Configuration

### Evidence 6024: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E24
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: A new rollout is triggered and a new revision of the deployment is created.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Observability

### Evidence 6034: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E34
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: However, when the rolling update strategy was used, the old replica set was scaled down one at a time, simultaneously scaling up the new replica set one at a time.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 6035: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E35
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Let's look at how a deployment performs an upgrade under the hood when a new deployment is created, say, to deploy five replicas, it first creates a replica set automatically, which in turn creates the number of pods required to meet the number of replicas.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 6036: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E36
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: When you upgrade your application, as we saw in the previous slide, the Kubernetes deployment object creates a new replica set under the hood and starts deploying the containers there.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Platform Engineering

### Evidence 6037: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E37

- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 6174: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/035_Demo - Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E122
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 035_Demo - Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: That basically means that the deployment is unable to pull the image from Docker Hub, and since it's unable to deploy any of the three images, any of the three pods, Kubernetes proactively stopped proceeding with the upgrade, and it proactively stopped terminating the old replica set because it knew that if it did terminate all of the old replica set and tried to deploy all new replicas, then the users would be impacted because the new image does not exist.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments
